M-051.NAME: mtproto-availability-diagnostics
M-051.path: backend/app/mtproto frontend scripts/phase39-mtproto-availability-smoke.mjs scripts/phase39-mtproto-live-smoke.sh scripts/phase40-official-mtproxy-live-smoke.sh scripts/phase41-kpproton-faketls-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-39.xml docs/plans/Phase-40.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml
M-051.owns: Secret-free MTProto availability proof from issued assignment through RU public 443, DE runtime acceptance, Telegram downstream reachability, req_pq proof, and user connection UX.
M-051.purpose: Close the remaining Telegram proxy unavailable state with deterministic diagnostics, safer user connection flow, and live proof before marking MTProto production-ready.

exports.phase39-live-smoke: Run an operator-safe issued-assignment availability smoke without printing tokens, proxy links, derived secrets, base secret, or salt.
exports.fake-tls-path-probe: Generate a Telegram-compatible fake-TLS ClientHello for one active assignment and prove RU-to-DE public route acceptance.
exports.telegram-downstream-probe: Verify DE runtime can reach at least one Telegram downstream from bootstrap config or safe static fallback.
exports.req-pq-path-probe: Use mtproto_proxy/mtp_ping or equivalent req_pq proof after runtime rebuild before claiming Telegram availability.
exports.owner-link-ux: Prefer a single Telegram open link and full-link copy flow in the user cabinet, with manual fields as secondary fallback.
exports.redacted-operator-diagnostics: Expose only masked SNI, assignment id, policy status, route status, and safe failure codes for admin/operator triage.

phase39-findings.finding-1: Live traces showed Telegram traffic reaches RU HAProxy and DE runtime, and SNI policy is loaded.
phase39-findings.finding-2: DE logs showed tls_invalid_digest during operator Telegram Desktop attempts, so the remaining blocker is end-to-end issued-link compatibility, stale client/link handling, or runtime handshake proof rather than basic routing.
phase39-findings.finding-3: Backend Python derivation and Erlang mtp_fake_tls derivation matched for the issued SNI, and an internal fake-TLS probe received a response through the public RU-to-DE route.
phase39-findings.finding-4: DE runtime had stale domain-fronting fallback value 127.0.0.1:8443 while Phase-38 contract requires private 9443 fallback.
phase39-findings.finding-5: 2026-05-19 follow-up diagnostics showed fake-TLS acceptance was insufficient: real req_pq timed out against the KPprotoN/Erlang data-plane.
phase39-findings.finding-6: 2026-05-19 temporary official MTProxy data-plane passed req_pq for official normal and secure secrets on the same DE host.
phase39-findings.finding-7: 2026-05-19 operator retest showed official MTProxy links can report available but remain unstable or unavailable for new users, so Phase-41 plans a return to the reference KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI model.
phase39-findings.finding-8: 2026-05-19 live Phase-41 diagnostics showed old mtproto_proxy e343e30 accepted fake-TLS and had valid Telegram downstream nonce reachability, but issued-link req_pq timed out; dependency lock moved to upstream 154a8fb for rebuild/retest.
phase39-findings.finding-9: 2026-05-19 operator clean-deploy validation confirmed the Phase-41 KPprotoN fake-TLS path works in Telegram Desktop/mobile after the upstream lock and non-empty ad-tag fix.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not rotate MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, user SNI, assignment ids, or existing tg links unless an explicit reissue phase is approved.
constraints.constraint-2: Diagnostics must never print full tg links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, wildcard private key, JWTs, or raw .env content.
constraints.constraint-3: Deploy/install scripts are not redesigned in this phase; only narrow guards for stale Phase-38 MTProto edge values are allowed.
constraints.constraint-4: User cabinet changes must remain compact on mobile and must not hide VPN trial/config actions.
constraints.constraint-5: Fake-TLS accept and downstream reachability do not satisfy production readiness without req_pq or real Telegram client proof.

acceptance.item-1: Operator can run one command to prove active assignment, policy apply, public RU route, DE fake-TLS accept, and Telegram downstream reachability with redacted output.
acceptance.item-2: User dashboard provides a primary Telegram connection action that avoids manual secret entry mistakes.
acceptance.item-3: DE runtime no longer preserves stale 8443 domain-fronting fallback after the Phase-39 guard is applied.
acceptance.item-4: Telegram Desktop/mobile availability proof is recorded before MTProto edge status is promoted from partial-live.
acceptance.item-5: Official MTProxy req_pq smoke is superseded as the production gate; Phase-41 requires KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI proof plus Telegram Desktop/mobile proof.

phase-41-plan.step-1: Add a redacted smoke proving issued ee fake-TLS owner payload shape, SNI route, DE policy apply, and no official runtime dependency.
phase-41-plan.step-2: Track whether adding a new user SNI changes policy without restarting the DE MTProto data-plane.
phase-41-plan.step-3: Keep final acceptance tied to real Telegram Desktop and phone connectivity, not policy health alone.

execution-evidence.item-1: Local Phase-39 shell syntax, static smoke, backend MTProto regression tests, frontend build, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint passed on 2026-05-18.
execution-evidence.item-2: Redacted live smoke found active assignment id 1, healthy policy_count 2, public RU:443 TCP route, fake-TLS accept, private DE fallback 9443, and reachable Telegram downstream config.
execution-evidence.item-3: The live DE stale fallback value 127.0.0.1:8443 was corrected to 127.0.0.1:9443 without rotating MTProto secrets or assignments.
execution-evidence.item-4: 2026-05-19 client-level diagnostics found req_pq timeout through KPprotoN/Erlang and selected Phase-40 official MTProxy as the closure path.
execution-evidence.item-5: 2026-05-19 Phase-41 verification design supersedes the official closure path after operator Telegram retests showed the official runtime does not satisfy stable product behavior.
execution-evidence.item-6: 2026-05-19 Phase-41 local smoke passed for ee owner payload shape, KPprotoN production route selection, official runtime inactivity, redaction, and static diagnostics.
execution-evidence.item-7: 2026-05-19 live fix diagnosis added req_pq evidence as the required closure gate above fake-TLS accept/downstream TCP reachability.
execution-evidence.item-8: 2026-05-19 operator clean-deploy validation confirmed VPN and Telegram Desktop/mobile MTProto proxy work from the issued Phase-41 link.

M-051.hotspot: H-038: Do not claim MTProto availability from policy health alone; issued-link and Telegram-client proof are required.
